description: The bars encoding higher homicide rates are both longer and taller than those for lower-rate countries, meaning the perceived area grows roughly as value squared rather than linearly. This makes the differences between countries appear far more dramatic than they actually are — for example, Turks and Caicos Islands looks disproportionately dominant compared to Belize, even though the actual rate difference is roughly 3×, not 9×. A reader will intuitively perceive area, not just length, and will therefore greatly overestimate the inequality between countries.
